system:
You are the Watchman of a legal AI lighthouse. Documents arrive in the watched folder; you decide what happens next. You read the filename and the first ~1500 characters, nothing more. Your job is triage, not analysis.

Output a single JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
  "documentType": "jv" | "nda" | "employment" | "lease" | "loan" | "saas" | "policy" | "other",
  "jurisdiction": "string — see jurisdiction rules below",
  "confidence": number,
  "urgency": "routine" | "elevated" | "crit
user:
FILENAME: xlitechnologies_12_02_2015.txt

=== CLIENT (the reviewer's home context — DO NOT copy into jurisdiction) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs

=== DOCUMENT TEXT (the source of facts — jurisdiction comes FROM HERE) ===
EXHIBIT 10.02 STRATEGIC ALLIANCE AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT is made on this 1st day of December, 2015 by and between BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability Company located at 3753 Howard Hughes Pkwy #200 Las Vegas NV 89169 (hereinafter "CLIENT"), and BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability Company located at 6795 Edmonds Street 3rd Floor, Las Vegas, NV 89118 (hereinafter "BOSCH"). 1. PARTIES: a) BOSCH TECHNOLOGIES, LLC ("BOSCH"); and b) BOSCH INTERNATIONAL, LLC ("CLIENT") 2. STRATEGIC ALLIANCE: Title of this relationship and agreement between BOSCH and CLIENT are detailed as follows: Both CLIENT and BOSCH act as two separate and individual companies operating independently of each other. BOSCH and CLIENT do not have any controlling interest of each other and are independently run and responsible for all of operations, reporting, taxes, liabilities and relationships. BOSCH reserves the right in keeping product generation and delivery confidential in which is not available for any type of audit. BOSCH reserves the right to share rights given unless it disrupts and/or interferes with CLIENTS business and/or productivity. 3. PRODUCT: "Product " BOSCH" has acquired, developed and continues to develop, a business using proprietary "Nano Printed Lights," and associated other products (the "Products"). BOSCH calls this Nano Printed Lights product "Printed LightSheets" which is considered the "Product Name". 4. PRODUCT NAME: "PRINTED LIGHT SHEETS" is the name a
=== END DOCUMENT TEXT ===

Return your JSON triage decision now. Jurisdiction must come from DOCUMENT TEXT above, never from CLIENT.

- Sole risk operations + the buy-back economics

=== CLAUSE 7: Clause ===
7. TERRITORY: United States of America and Canada, excluding the US Virgin Islands 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
=== END CLAUSE 7 ===

The CLIENT block you will receive (company, industry, jurisdiction, concerns) tells you HOW STRICTLY to read the document. It is the client's stance, not the document's facts. The summary, documentType, and risks must describe what is in the DOCUMENT — not what the client does or where the client i

=== DOCUMENT (the source of facts) ===
Filename: xlitechnologies_12_02_2015.txt

=== CLIENT STANCE (use ONLY for risk framing — NOT as document facts) ===
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
NOTE: the client reviewing this document may or may not be a party to it. If you need to refer to the reviewer in the summary or in risk descriptions, use "the client" — NEVER a specific company name. Specific company names come ONLY from the per-clause findings.

=== PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS FROM THE TEAM (the source of truth for parties, jurisdiction, subject matter) ===
cl 1 (Clause) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause defines the parties' roles and operating structure, but lacks detail on financial risk management mechanisms for the non-operator.
  [major] The clause lacks detail on financial risk management mechanisms for the non-operator in case of unforeseen circumstances. This could lead to financial strain for the non-operator if a capital call (effec (effectiveness 20%, seen 1x) is not handled transparently.

cl 3 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the 'Product' and its name, but lacks details on joint venture responsibilities, financial obligations, or risk allocation.
  [major] The clause lacks details on joint venture responsibilities, financial obligations, or risk allocation.

cl 4 (Clause) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines the product name, but lacks detail on how this product will be marketed or managed by the non-operator.
  [minor] This clause lacks detail on how this product will be marketed or managed by the non-operator.

cl 5 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause defines the scope of distribution rights for the non-operator, but lacks detail on financial obligations or risk mitigation mechanisms for the non-operator's potential losses.
  [minor] The clause lacks detail on financial obligations or risk mitigation mechanisms for the non-operator's potential losses.

cl 6 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause grants exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product, potentially limiting Client's ability to pursue alternative business strategies or negotiate with other parties.
  [major] This clause grants exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product, potentially limiting Client's ability to pursue alternative business strategies or negotiate with other parties.

cl 7 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause outlines the scope of rights and responsibilities for both parties in a joint venture agreement. It specifically addresses territory, reserved rights, cost allocation, and assistance to the client.
  [major] The clause grants BOSCH exclusive rights to operate within the specified territory and limits CLIENT's ability to act independently. This could create a risk of conflict if BOSCH's actions negatively impact CLIENT's business objectives.
  [major] The clause outlines the responsibility for costs and expenses, but it lacks clarity on how capital calls will be handled in case of unforeseen circumstances. This could create financial risk for the non-operator if a capital call (effec (effectiveness 20%, seen 1x) is not properly addressed.
  [major] The clause grants BOSCH exclusive rights to operate within the specified territory and limits CLIENT's ability to act independently. This could create a risk of conflict if BOSCH's actions negatively impact CLIENT's business objectives.

cl 9 (Clause) — favours operator
  summary: This clause imposes joint and several indemnification obligations on CLIENT, potentially exposing them to significant financial risk in the event of claims against BOSCH or its affiliates.
  [major] CLIENT is exposed to joint and several indemnification for claims against BOSCH's affiliates. This could expose CLIENT to significant financial risk in the event of a claim, particularly if the claim involves multiple parties or substantial damages.

Now produce the executive synthesis JSON. Rules:
  - Parties, jurisdiction, and document type come from the PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS only.
  - Names of parties: copy them VERBATIM from the per-clause findings, whatever they are. Do not substitute company names from your prior knowledge. Do not invent.

summary: This contract defines the roles and responsibilities of CLIENT and BOSCH for a joint venture agreement regarding the Product, including territory rights, cost allocation, and product distribution. The agreement grants BOSCH exclusive rights to operate within the specified territory and limits CLIENT's ability to act independently. This could create a risk of conflict if BOSCH's actions negatively impact CLIENT's business objectives.

clauses.title: 1. Clause
clauses.text: This agreement details the relationship between BOSCH and CLIENT as two separate companies operating independently of each other. BOSCH reserves the right to keep product generation and delivery confidential, limiting audit access. BOSCH also retains the right to share rights unless it disrupts or interferes with CLIENT's business.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks detail on financial risk management mechanisms for the non-operator in case of unforeseen circumstances. This could lead to financial strain for the non-operator if a capital call (effec (effectiveness 20%, seen 1x) is not handled transparently.

clauses.title: 3. Clause
clauses.text: This clause defines the 'Product' and its name, BOSCH has acquired, developed and continues to develop a business using proprietary "Nano Printed Lights", and associated other products (the "Products"). BOSCH calls this Nano Printed Lights product "Printed LightSheets" which is considered the "Product Name".
clauses.concern: The clause lacks details on joint venture responsibilities, financial obligations, or risk allocation.

clauses.title: 4. Clause
clauses.text: 4. PRODUCT NAME: "PRINTED LIGHT SHEETS" is the name and title CLIENT will use to market for Nano Printed Lights.
clauses.concern: This clause lacks detail on how this product will be marketed or managed by the non-operator.

clauses.title: 5. Clause
clauses.text: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
clauses.concern: The clause lacks detail on financial obligations or risk mitigation mechanisms for the non-operator's potential losses.

clauses.title: 6. Clause
clauses.text: Bosch hereby grants to Client the exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product, subject to the Territory as set forth below, to certain select companies in the Automotive Industry, each of which shall be approved by Bosch in writing as requested by the Client on a case by case basis.
clauses.concern: This clause grants exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product, potentially limiting Client's ability to pursue alternative business strategies or negotiate with other parties.

clauses.title: 7. Clause
clauses.text: 7. TERRITORY: United States of America and Canada, excluding the US Virgin Islands 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory.
clauses.concern: The clause grants BOSCH exclusive rights to operate within the specified territory and limits CLIENT's ability to act independently. This could create a risk of conflict if BOSCH's actions negatively impact CLIENT's business objectives.

clauses.title: 9. Clause
clauses.text: CLIENT agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless BOSCH and its parents, subsidiary and affiliated entities, successors, assigns and licensees, and their respective officers, agents, directors, owners, shareholders and employees, harmless from any and all claims, actions or proceedings of any kind and from any and all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) relating to or arising out of any claim by a third Party.
clauses.concern: CLIENT is exposed to joint and several indemnification for claims against BOSCH's affiliates. This could expose CLIENT to significant financial risk in the event of a claim, particularly if the claim involves multiple parties or substantial damages.

clauses.title: 10. Clause
clauses.text: 10. CONFIDENTIALITY: CLIENT acknowledges that irreparable injury and damage will result from the disclosure to any third party of Proprietary Information associated with the Product and agrees (1) not to disclose any Proprietary Information to any third party, and (2) not to allow any third party to possess, handle, disassemble, touch, photograph, film or otherwise record images of the Product while the Product is upon CLIENT'S premises or in CLIENT'S possession, custody or control if the product is or has not been purchased with a purchase order and payment fulfilled.
clauses.concern: The clause imposes strict confidentiality obligations on the non-operator regarding proprietary information related to the joint venture's product. This could limit the non-operator's ability to utilize or share this information, potentially impacting their business operations and decision-making.

clauses.title: 11. Clause
clauses.text: 11. ENTIRE AGREEMENT: This Strategic Alliance Agreement "Agreement", between BOSCH and CLIENT as to the subject matter hereof, and supersede all previous agreements, warranties or representations, oral or written, which may have been made between Studio and Licensor as to the subject matter hereof. By signing in the spaces provided below BOSCH and CLIENT accept and agree to all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
clauses.concern: This clause establishes the entire agreement between BOSCH and CLIENT, superseding any prior agreements. It is a standard boilerplate clause that does not raise specific risk concerns.

risks.description: The agreement grants BOSCH exclusive rights to operate within the specified territory and limits CLIENT's ability to act independently. This could create a risk of conflict if BOSCH's actions negatively impact CLIENT's business objectives.

The agreement grants BOSCH exclusive rights to operate within the specified territory and limits CLIENT's ability to act independently. This could create a risk of conflict if BOSCH's actions negatively impact CLIENT's business objectives.

The agreement imposes joint and several indemnification obligations on CLIENT, potentially exposing them to significant financial risk in the event of claims against BOSCH or its affiliates. This could expose CLIENT to significant financial risk in the event of a claim, particularly if the claim involves multiple parties or substantial damages.

recommendations: Issue a formal demand for clarification on the specific terms of the agreement regarding capital calls and financial risk management mechanisms, including the non-operator's responsibilities.
Demand that BOSCH provide detailed information about their proposed marketing and distribution strategy for the Product to ensure alignment with CLIENT's business objectives.
Audit the existing legal framework for joint ventures in NSW to identify potential risks and opportunities.
